question: For the quarterly online platform data, what can be concluded about the pattern of 'Online Searches' from this 100% stacked bar chart?

options.A: The absolute number of online searches increases every quarter because the share is larger in later quarters.
options.B: Online searches show a steady increase in both share and absolute numbers across all quarters.
options.C: The chart normalizes unrelated categories to 100% per quarter, so it only shows the share of online searches and cannot reveal their absolute trend over time.
options.D: Online searches have the same absolute value in every quarter because each bar reaches 100%.

explanation: The chart normalizes unrelated categories to 100% within each quarter. It can suggest that the share of a metric is higher in later quarters, but it cannot establish an absolute increasing trend without the underlying totals. A higher percentage share does not necessarily mean a higher absolute quantity.
